text.paragraph:
FNBR 40369438 Date(s): 3/2/22 - 3/2/22
Virginia Mason Issaquah 100 NE Gilman Blvd Issaquah, WA 98027- US (425) 557-8000
Discharge Disposition: *Home (01)

Encounter Type: Clinic -Z Office Visit

Location: DT LIN Allergy

Location: UV Video Visit

tr.td:
albuterol (albuterol CFC free 90 mcg/inh inhalation aerosol)
Status: Ordered
Start Date: 9/22/25
2 Inhalation Oral Inhalation Every 4 Hours as needed cough, wheeze, or difficulty breathing; take with spacer. Refills: 0.
Ordering provider: Soong MD, Derrick L

beclomethasone (Qvar Redihaler 40 mcg/inh inhalation aerosol)
Status: Ordered
Start Date: 9/22/25
2 Inhalation Oral Inhalation Twice Daily. rinse mouth and throat after use. Refills: 0.
Ordering provider: Soong MD, Derrick L

Supplies (Spacer for MDI)
Status: Ordered
Start Date: 8/28/23
1 Each Oral Inhalation One Time. As directed with MDI. Refills: 0.
Ordering provider: Soong MD, Derrick L

item.paragraph:
Morrison MD, John C: PERFORM
Event Display: PRIMARY CARE Outpt Clinic Note
Authored Date: 20220302184333-0800

CHEN, LAURENCE M
DOB:11/09/2017
MRN:7447565
Visit Date:03/02/2022
Assessment/Plan
_
1. Cough
We discussed that he likely has a viral respiratory illness. Family says they do have home rapid COVID-19 test, and will administer one to him. They will call nurse tomorrow if the test is positive for advice about isolation. Otherwise can continue albuterol every 4-6 hours as needed. Advised follow-up for in-person appointment in 2 days if not improving, or sooner if worsening.
TeleVideo Addendum
Patient was seen today via a Primary Care Televideo visit.
Synchronous live secure, HIPAA compliant audio/video functionality was used to conduct the visit.
Patient Location/Originating Site: Home
Other Family Members, Caregivers, etc with Patient at time of visit: Patient's mother and father
Provider Location/Distant Site: Virginia Mason Issaquah
Chief Complaint
COUGH X 2 DAYS HX RAD
History of Present Illness
Patient is a 4-year-old boy a whose parents consent to video visit for evaluation of cough that has been present for 3 days. Parents say his symptoms are unchanged from onset. He did have a fever to 102 degrees F yesterday. Also with nasal congestion and rhinorrhea. They denies shortness of breath. They have been giving him albuterol every 4-6 hours with improvement, and he remains active and playful after treatment he does have a history of asthma in the past, and is not on inhaled corticosteroid.
Review of Systems
Common Review of Systems and Pain Documentation
Common ROS Symptoms
Event Name
Event Result
ROS Chills
No
ROS Cough
Yes
ROS Ear pain
No
ROS Increased temperature (fever)
Yes
ROS Hx chronic sinusitis/nasal polyps
No
ROS Hx of antibiotic use within 3 mo
No
ROS Nausea/vomiting/diarrhea (specify)
No
ROS Onset of symptoms
3 days
ROS Pregnant or breastfeeding
No
ROS SOB - check pulse ox/rr
No
ROS Sore throat
No
ROS Wheezing
Yes
Condition Specific Patient Reported Review of Systems
Acute Respiratory Illness ROS BPTF
Event Name
Event Result
ROS Flu vaccine this year in flu season
No
ROS Hx reactive airways/asthma/emphysema
No
ROS Ill contacts
Yes
ROS Muscle aches
No
ROS Nasal congestion
No
ROS Nasal discharge
Yes
ROS Nausea/vomiting/diarrhea (specify)
No
ROS Post-nasal drip/clearing throat
No
ROS Rash
No
ROS Sinus/tooth pain
No
ROS Sore throat
No
Asthma ROS BPTF
Event Name
Event Result
ROS Hx chronic sinusitis/nasal polyps
No
Physical Exam
No apparent respiratory distress; active and playful on video feed.
External Vitals & Measurements
External Weight (lb): 32 lb (03/02/22)
External Weight (oz): 4 oz (03/02/22)
External Weight Obtained By: Patient estimated (03/02/22)
Safety and Function
Fall Risk and Activities of Daily Living
No qualifying data available.
Cognitive Assessments
VMCS/MOCA Results
No qualifying data available.
Behavioral Health
Behavioral Health Scores and Assessments
No qualifying data available.
Problem List/Past Medical History
Ongoing
Abdominal pain
Birth mark Comments: forehead
Chromosomal microduplication
Hemangioma
Prematurity
Strabismus
Historical
No qualifying data
Medications
Inpatient
No active inpatient medications
Home
Albuterol (Eqv-ProAir HFA) 90 mcg/inh inhalation aerosol, 1 puff(s), ORAL INH, Q6 Hours, PRN
Vitamin D, Daily
Allergies
NKA
Social History
Home/Environment
Lives with Father, Mother, Siblings, Grandmother.
Family History
Allergies: Mother and Father.
Anxiety: Mother.
Depression: Mother.
Diabetes mellitus type 2: Sister.
Eczema: Father and Pat. GFather.
Health Maintenance
Pending (in the next year)
There are no current recommendations pending
Due In Future
IMM Influenza Vaccine (6mo-100yrs) not due until 08/24/22 and every 200 day(s)
PED Well Child Check (2-18 yrs) not due until 11/11/22 and every 1 year(s)
Satisfied (in the past 1 year)
Satisfied
IMM Influenza Vaccine (6mo-100yrs) on 10/01/21. Satisfied by Nguyen RN, Jade H
PED Well Child Check (2-18 yrs) on 11/11/21.
(Electronically Signed on 03/02/22 06:43 PM)
Morrison MD, John C
